executive_summary: New frameworks for whole-body mobile manipulation (AutoMoMa, WHOLE-MoMa, FastGrasp) emphasize scalable trajectory generation, data-efficient offline RL, and fast, impact-robust grasping with tactile feedback.

Humanoid and dexterous manipulation advances (HTD, XRZero-G0, VGA) highlight touch-centered multimodal policies, large-scale robot-free VR demonstrations, and 3D vision-geometry backbones that outperform vision-language models.

Legged loco-manipulation for cooperative transport (PAINT) shows that proprioceptive intent estimation can replace explicit force sensing and generalize across terrains, partners, and robot embodiments.
New motion-planning and control methods (UGE-MPC, Marble, RL-based dynamics refinement) tackle exploration, contact-rich LCQPs, and bridging first-order hybrid plans to physically feasible execution.

Continuum and long-horizon manipulation work (reconfigurable TDCM actuation, screw-theoretic lab skills, nanoparticle synthesis automation) focuses on model-free shape control and re-usable, constraint-aware skills from single demonstrations.

institution_matches.title: PAINT: Partner-Agnostic Intent-Aware Cooperative Transport with Legged Robots
institution_matches.overview: Introduces PAINT, a hierarchical learning framework enabling legged robots to cooperatively transport payloads by inferring partner intent purely from proprioceptive signals. A high-level intent estimator is decoupled from a terrain-robust locomotion backbone, yielding compliant, partner-agnostic transport that scales to multi-robot settings and different robot embodiments without external force sensing.

institution_matches.title: Dynamic Multi-Robot Task Allocation under Uncertainty and Communication Constraints: A Game-Theoretic Approach
institution_matches.overview: Proposes Iterative Best Response (IBR), a decentralized task-allocation policy for fleets of robots operating under uncertain task completion, deadlines, and sparse communication. In large-scale drone delivery scenarios, IBR achieves competitive completion rates with lower computational cost compared to classical scheduling and conflict-based allocation baselines.

institution_matches.title: RMGS-SLAM: Real-time Multi-sensor Gaussian Splatting SLAM
institution_matches.overview: Presents a tightly-coupled LiDAR–inertial–visual SLAM system that maintains a real-time 3D Gaussian splatting map while estimating robot pose in large-scale environments. A cascaded Gaussian initialization strategy and Gaussian-based loop closure enable a favorable balance of efficiency, localization accuracy, and photorealistic rendering.

institution_matches.title: Machine Learning-Based Real-Time Detection of Compensatory Trunk Movements Using Trunk-Wrist Inertial Measurement Units
institution_matches.overview: Uses just two IMUs (trunk and wrist) with an XGBoost classifier to detect compensatory trunk movements during upper-limb tasks in real time. The approach matches optical-motion-capture performance on able-bodied subjects and shows promising, though variable, generalization to clinical populations, supporting sparse wearable monitoring in therapy.

institution_matches.title: 3DRO: Lidar-level SE(3) Direct Radar Odometry Using a 2D Imaging Radar and a Gyroscope
institution_matches.overview: Extends planar direct radar odometry to full SE(3) by fusing 2D imaging radar velocity estimates with 3D gyroscope integration over SO(3). Evaluated over 643 km from Boreas-RT, the method attains lidar-comparable odometry accuracy with a simple, radar–IMU configuration.

institution_matches.title: ReefMapGS: Enabling Large-Scale Underwater Reconstruction by Closing the Loop Between Multimodal SLAM and Gaussian Splatting
institution_matches.overview: Integrates multimodal SLAM with 3D Gaussian splatting to incrementally reconstruct complex underwater reef environments without COLMAP. The system seeds reconstruction from high-certainty trajectory segments, alternates local 3DGS optimization with pose-graph refinement, and achieves accurate, large-scale AUV mapping over hundreds of meters.

institution_matches.title: Artificial Intelligence for Modeling and Simulation of Mixed Automated and Human Traffic
institution_matches.overview: Surveys AI methods for simulating traffic with both autonomous and human-driven vehicles, organizing work into agent-level behavior, environment-level simulation, and cognitive/physics-informed models. It critiques current simulators’ reliance on simplistic drivers and outlines research directions, metrics, and datasets for more realistic mixed-autonomy traffic evaluation.

topic_matches.title: Learning Versatile Humanoid Manipulation with Touch Dreaming
topic_matches.overview: Develops an RL-based whole-body humanoid controller and a VR teleoperation pipeline to collect rich loco-manipulation demonstrations. The proposed Humanoid Transformer with Touch Dreaming treats tactile signals as a core modality, predicting future contact forces and tactile latents to enable robust, contact-aware execution on diverse real-world tasks like folding, insertion, and tool use.

topic_matches.title: FastGrasp: Learning-based Whole-body Control method for Fast Dexterous Grasping with Mobile Manipulators
topic_matches.overview: Introduces FastGrasp, a two-stage RL framework that couples grasp candidate generation via a conditional VAE on point clouds with a learned whole-body controller for high-speed base–arm–hand coordination. Tactile sensing is used online to stabilize impacts and adapt to object variation, yielding robust fast grasping in both simulation and real-world tests.

topic_matches.title: Scalable Trajectory Generation for Whole-Body Mobile Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: Presents AutoMoMa, a GPU-accelerated trajectory optimization framework that unifies base–arm–object kinematics into a single chain and parallelizes planning for whole-body mobile manipulation. It generates over 500k physically valid trajectories across many scenes and robots, showing that large-scale, high-fidelity data is key for training generalizable imitation-learning policies.

topic_matches.title: Whole-Body Mobile Manipulation using Offline Reinforcement Learning on Sub-optimal Controllers
topic_matches.overview: Proposes WHOLE-MoMa, which uses a randomized, sub-optimal whole-body controller to collect diverse data, then applies offline RL with a chunked diffusion policy and Q-chunking critic to improve coordination. On articulated-object manipulation tasks with a TIAGo++ mobile manipulator, policies surpass WBC, behavior cloning, and offline RL baselines and transfer to hardware without real-world training data.

topic_matches.title: XRZero-G0: Pushing the Frontier of Dexterous Robotic Manipulation with Interfaces, Quality and Ratios
topic_matches.overview: Introduces XRZero-G0, a VR-based, robot-free data collection system with ergonomic dual grippers and a closed-loop pipeline for quality-controlled dexterous manipulation datasets. Experiments show that mixing a small amount of real-robot data with a much larger volume of robot-free demonstrations (e.g., 10:1) can match fully robot-collected datasets while reducing acquisition cost by ~20×.

topic_matches.title: Robotic Manipulation is Vision-to-Geometry Mapping ($f(v) \rightarrow G$): Vision-Geometry Backbones over Language and Video Models
topic_matches.overview: Argues that manipulation policies should be built on 3D vision-geometry backbones rather than vision-language or video encoders, and proposes the Vision-Geometry-Action (VGA) model using a pretrained 3D world model. With progressive volumetric modulation and joint training, VGA outperforms strong VLA baselines and shows robust zero-shot viewpoint generalization in real-world tasks.

topic_matches.title: Actuation space reduction to facilitate insightful shape matching in a novel reconfigurable tendon driven continuum manipulator
topic_matches.overview: Presents a reconfigurable tendon-driven continuum manipulator where spacer disks can be rotated to reroute tendons even post-actuation, greatly expanding shape control. By working in curvature–torsion space, the method identifies influential disks and uses a sequential, model-free shape-matching strategy to achieve desired backbones without complex modeling.

topic_matches.title: From Kinematics to Dynamics: Learning to Refine Hybrid Plans for Physically Feasible Execution
topic_matches.overview: Addresses the gap between first-order hybrid temporal plans and dynamically feasible trajectories by framing plan refinement as an RL problem in continuous space with explicit second-order constraints. The learned policy adjusts the initial kinematic plan to satisfy physical limits like accelerations and deadlines, reliably recovering executable motions.

topic_matches.title: Robotic Nanoparticle Synthesis via Solution-based Processes
topic_matches.overview: Automates long-horizon, solution-based nanoparticle synthesis by representing manipulation skills like pouring and knob-turning as sequences of constant screw motions learned from a single demonstration. This screw-theoretic, coordinate-invariant representation captures geometric constraints and supports robust reuse and adaptation of skills across lab setups.

topic_matches.title: Uncertainty Guided Exploratory Trajectory Optimization for Sampling-Based Model Predictive Control
topic_matches.institution: Robotics, Sensing and Networks Laboratory (RSN).; University of Minnesota, Twin Cities; University of Texas at Austin
topic_matches.overview: Introduces UGE-TO, which models trajectories as uncertainty ellipsoids and enforces separation in distribution space via Hellinger distance to promote diverse exploration during optimization. Embedded into MPC as UGE-MPC, it achieves faster convergence and higher success rates than standard sampling-based controllers under the same sample budget, particularly in cluttered environments.
